arts.ttl: ‘Great squad’ – Reading boss Noel Hunt has say on Birmingham City
arts.sum: Birmingham City have built a ‘great squad’ for themselves over recent times, Reading boss Noel Hunt has said.… The post ‘Great squad’ – Reading boss Noel Hunt has say on Birmingham City appeared first on The72 - Football League News.

Reading have recovered from a recent blip to get themselves right back into play-off contention with the gap to sixth place just four points. 11 of the Royals' 14 League One wins have come here, but ...

arts.ttl: EFL record NOT part of Reading team talk ahead of Birmingham City clash
arts.sum: Reading boss Noel Hunt is not going to use the club's 106-point record as part of his team-talk for tomorrow's meeting with runaway League One leaders Birmingham City. In 2005/06, the Royals lost only two Championship matches and picked up 106 points on route to the Premier League for the first time. Still an EFL record to this day, many clubs have tried- and failed- to break the record, with Hunt's brother Stephen a key member of Steve Coppell's squad 19 years ago. However, Chris Davies' Blues- on a 16 match unbeaten run- are on track to pick up 109 points and smash through the tally set by the Royals. Back in 2023, while interim manager as the club suffered relegation to League One, Hunt admitted that this was part of his team talk when Vincent Kompany's Burnley visited the SCL Stadium as the Clarets still looked on course to bypass the tally in April.

arts.ttl: Preview: Reading vs. Birmingham City - prediction, team news, lineups
arts.sum: Sports Mole previews Saturday's League One clash between Reading and Birmingham City, including predictions, team news and possible lineups.

arts.ttl: 'It's still on our radar': Reading boss confirms interest in free agent addition
arts.sum: Reading manager Noel Hunt has confirmed that the club are still on the look out for a free agent to help boost the squad between now and the end of the season. The Royals lost Sam Smith to Wrexham in January and goalkeeper Dean Bouzanis agreed to mutually end his time in Berkshire. As Burton Albion's Billy Bodin was the only arrival, on loan until the summer, the club still have space in their squad for a free addition. Reading have 16 League One matches remaining and ahead of tomorrow's visit from League One leaders Birmingham City sit four points outside the play-off places. Speaking ahead of the clash with Blues, Hunt said: "It's still on the radar. We have got scope to maybe bring in one. It’s important that we bring in the right character for the group. We are a young group, so it has to be the right person, first and foremost, with the same beliefs in terms of work rate, out of possession stuff and quality in possession.

arts.ttl: Jay Stansfield 'miss' worry as Birmingham City issue major update
arts.sum: When he was carried off on a stretcher after a tough tackle in Birmingham's 2-1 win over Bradford City, Blues fans were increasingly worried about talisman and goal machine Jay Stansfield's health. Former Birmingham City captain Ian Clarkson has spoken about Jay Stansfield's injury and his importance to Blues

arts.ttl: Tony Mowbray reveals his Birmingham City endorsement as he prepares for familiar test
arts.sum: Tony Mowbray will cross swords with Gary Rowett at The Hawthorns this weekend - the man who he recommended to Birmingham City 12 months ago after being forced to step away from his managerial role. Tony Mowbray was forced out of his Birmingham City job a year ago because of ill health and he was deputised by Gary Rowett

arts.ttl: Reading captain potentially 90 minutes away from league return after 10 months out
arts.sum: Reading captain Andy Yiadom could be just one 90 minute performance away from a return to first-team action after 10 months out, manager Noel Hunt has confirmed. The 33-year-old has over 200 Royals appearances to his name since arriving in 2018 but has been unable to feature since a 2-0 win at Bristol Rovers in April 2024 due to injury. After a 45-minute return for the Under-21s at Aston Villa and a 60-minute cameo at Sunderland on Monday, the former Ghana international could make a return to a first-team squad this month, should he play the full 90 minutes against West Ham United on Monday. Speaking ahead of Saturday's clash with League One leaders Birmingham City, Hunt said: "He is still ticking a few boxes, but he is feeling good. We will have a look- there is an Under-21s game on Monday night- and try to get him 90 minutes. After that, I think he’s fair game to come into the squad.

arts.ttl: Reading commercial chief on 'at-risk fans' and reassuring Dai Yongge claim
arts.sum: Head of Commercial at Reading Football Club, Tim Kilpatrick, has reiterated that controversial owner Dai Yongge has 'never taken money out of the club' despite supporters' fears. The businessman, who first purchased the club in 2017, has been the subject of protests for the past two years as attempts to sell the club appear in vein. Many salaries and bills have failed to be paid by the billionaire, with player sales used last year as a way of funding the club. Star striker Sam Smith was sold to Wrexham in January, with the club admitting the fee was too good to turn down, but many supporters fear the owner will look to take any cash left in the club for his own personal use. Speaking to popular fan channel Elm Park Royals, Mr Kilpatrick has confirmed that this is not, and never has been, the case.

arts.ttl: BOOK REVIEW: Sainty was happy to play for £40 per week at Reading
arts.sum: WHEN Reading FC came calling with an offer of £40 per week plus a £1,000 signing-on fee and help towards a new £5,000 house, the young player described it as a no-brainer. He considered it better than £25 a week to stay at Tottenham Hotspur and play in their reserves. You would have guessed by now that we are not talking about a modern-day footballer. This happened a long way back, in 1967. The player in question was John Sainty - nicknamed The Saint by fans at a time when Reading played at Elm Park and were known as the Biscuitmen. His life story, a part autobiography, has just been published with the title John Sainty, Last Man Standing - from Saint to Saints and Beyond.
